---
title: "figure4_heatmap"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "11/12/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggraph)
library(igraph)
library(Hmisc)
library(writexl)

library(tidygraph)
library(ppcor)

library(reshape2)
library(ComplexHeatmap)

```


#demographic
```{r}
food_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQA862_new.xlsx")
nutrition_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQB800.xlsx")
species = read_xlsx("data/species_866.xlsx")
metadata = read_xlsx("data/ethnicity_866.xlsx")

food_data <- food_data %>%
  mutate(across(c("Mushrooms", "Chapati"), as.numeric))
  #dplyr::select(-c("Cruciferous_veg", "Noodle_dishes","Rice_dishes"))
food_data 
nutrition_data
species
metadata
food_data
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# 1. Remove the sample ID column (first column)
species_mat <- species[, -1]

# 2. Compute total abundance for each species across all samples
species_totals <- colSums(species_mat, na.rm = TRUE)

# 3. Convert to dataframe
species_ranked <- data.frame(
  Species = names(species_totals),
  TotalAbundance = species_totals
) %>%
  arrange(desc(TotalAbundance))

# 4. Extract top 10
top10_species <- species_ranked %>% slice(2:11)

top10_species$Species

```


```{r}
colnames(species)[1] = "Participant_ID"
colnames(metadata)[1] = "Participant_ID"
threshold <- nrow(species) * 0.5

#species_filtered1 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > nrow(species) * 0.2)
#species_filtered1
#species_filtered2 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)

#abundance_threshold <- 0.0001
#species_filtered2 <- species_filtered %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x) > (abundance_threshold * sum(species)))
#species_filtered2
species_filtered2 <- species_filtered2[, !names(species_filtered2) %in% c("unclassified", "others", "Homo sapiens")]
#species_filtered2$`Homo sapiens`
#species_filtered2

cols_to_fix <- setdiff(names(food_data), "Participant_ID")
cols_to_fix
food_data[cols_to_fix][is.na(food_data[cols_to_fix])] <- 0
# food_data[is.na(food_data)] <- 0
#food_data <- data.frame(lapply(food_data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
nutrition_data[is.na(nutrition_data)] <- 0
```



```{r}

unique_species <- read.csv("data/ethnic_species_anova.csv")
target_species = top10_species$Species
# target_species <- c(
#   "Faecalibacterium prausnitzii",
#   "Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron",
#   "Bacteroides fragilis",
#   "Bifidobacterium adolescentis",
#   "Roseburia hominis",
#   "Collinsella aerofaciens",
#   "Methanobrevibacter smithii",
#   "Akkermansia muciniphila"
# 
# )
combined_species <- c(unique_species$species, target_species)

# remove duplicates if you only want each species once
combined_species <- unique(combined_species)

# see result
combined_species

species
sig_species <- species %>%
  dplyr::select("Participant_ID", all_of(combined_species))
sig_species
```


```{r}
food_data
nutrition_data
sig_species
metadata
```
```{r}
merged_data <- food_data %>%
  inner_join(sig_species, by = "Participant_ID") %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

# 2. Calculate partial correlations adjusting for confounders
# Initialize matrices to store correlations and p-values
species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
food_cols <- colnames(food_data)[colnames(food_data) != "Participant_ID"]
cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(food_cols))
p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(food_cols))

for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
  for(j in 1:length(food_cols)) {
    # Create dummy variables for ethnicity using the correct column name
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      species = rank(merged_data[[species_cols[i]]]),  
      food = rank(merged_data[[food_cols[j]]]),        
      gender = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
      age = rank(merged_data$FREG8_Age),              
      bmi = rank(merged_data$BMI),                    
      ethnic_C = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C"),  # Updated column name
      ethnic_I = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I"),
      ethnic_M = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M")
    )
    
    pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$food, 
                            temp_data[,c("gender", "age", "bmi", 
                                       "ethnic_C", "ethnic_I", "ethnic_M")])
    
    cor_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$estimate
    p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
  }
}

# Convert matrices to data frames
rownames(cor_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(cor_matrix) <- food_cols
rownames(p_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(p_matrix) <- food_cols

# Create annotation for significant correlations
sig_matrix <- p_matrix < 0.05
stars <- ifelse(sig_matrix, "*", "")
```

```{r}
species_dist <- dist(cor_matrix)
species_clust <- hclust(species_dist)
species_order <- species_clust$labels[species_clust$order]

# For food groups (columns)
food_dist <- dist(t(cor_matrix))
food_clust <- hclust(food_dist)
food_order <- food_clust$labels[food_clust$order]
food_order
# Convert to long format with ordered factors
# melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)
# colnames(melted_cor) <- c("Species", "Food", "Correlation")
# melted_cor$Significant <- melted_p$value < 0.05
# 
# # Set the order based on clustering
# melted_cor$Species <- factor(melted_cor$Species, levels = species_order)
# melted_cor$Food <- factor(melted_cor$Food, levels = food_order)
# 
# # Create custom color palette
# extended_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)
# 
# # Create the plot with italicized species names
# ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Food, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
#   geom_tile() +
#   scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(extended_palette), 
#                       limits = c(-0.15, 0.15),
#                       oob = scales::squish) +
#   geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
#             color = "black", size = 5) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   theme(
#     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
#     axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),  # Italicize species names
#     axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
#     legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
#   ) +
#   labs(
#     title = "Spearman Correlations between Species and Food Groups",
#     subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and BMI\n* indicates p < 0.05",
#     x = "Food Groups",
#     y = "Species"
#   )
# #ggsave("figure/figure4_correlation_heatmap.png", width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
# Previous code remains the same until the melting part
food_order = c("Eggs", "Noodle_dishes", "Cruciferous_veg", "Leafy_veg", "Poultry", "Seafood", "Red_meat", "Pork", "Mushrooms", "Rice_dishes", "Fruits", "Chapati", "Legumes", "Dairy", "Tea", "Coffee", "Root_veg", "Lontong", "Idli_Thosai", "Bakery_bread"  )
food_order_rev <- rev(food_order)
# Melt both matrices
melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)
melted_p <- melt(p_matrix)
colnames(melted_cor) <- c("Species", "Food", "Correlation")
colnames(melted_p) <- c("Species", "Food", "P_value")

# Combine the melted data
melted_data <- melted_cor %>%
  mutate(Significant = melted_p$P_value < 0.05)

# Set the order based on clustering
melted_data$Species <- factor(melted_data$Species, levels = species_order)
melted_data$Food <- factor(melted_data$Food, levels = food_order_rev)

# Create custom color palette
extended_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)
# red_species <- c("Faecalibacterium prausnitzii", "Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron", 
#                  "Bacteroides fragilis", "Bifidobacterium adolescentis", 
#                  "Roseburia hominis", "Collinsella aerofaciens", 
#                  "Methanobrevibacter smithii", "Akkermansia muciniphila")
# Create the plot with italicized species names
ggplot(melted_data, aes(x = Food, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(extended_palette), 
                      limits = c(-0.15, 0.15),
                      oob = scales::squish) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
            color = "black", size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
                              #colour = ifelse(levels(melted_data$Species) %in% red_species, "red", "black")), 
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Spearman Correlations between Species and Food Groups",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, Ethnicity and BMI\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Food Groups",
    y = "Species"
  )

#ggsave("figure/figure3/figure3_anova_select6.pdf", width = 12, height = 10)
```
```{r}
library(openxlsx)
cor_results <- melted_cor %>%
  left_join(melted_p, by = c("Species", "Food"))

# Optional: keep significance column
cor_results <- cor_results %>%
  mutate(Significant = P_value < 0.05)

# Save to Excel
write.xlsx(cor_results,
           file = "data/Sup1_correlation_results.xlsx",
           sheetName = "Cor_Pvals",
           rowNames = FALSE)
```




## suplymentary
```{r}
melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)
melted_p <- melt(p_matrix)
colnames(melted_cor) <- c("Species", "Food", "Correlation")
colnames(melted_p) <- c("Species", "Food", "P_value")

# Combine the melted data as it was done in your original code
melted_data <- melted_cor %>%
  mutate(Significant = melted_p$P_value < 0.05)

# Add additional columns for formatting
melted_data <- melted_data %>%
  mutate(
         # Add P_value from melted_p
         P_value = melted_p$P_value,
         # Format correlation values with 2 decimal places
         Correlation_formatted = sprintf("%.2f", Correlation),
         # Format p-values with scientific notation for small values
         P_value_formatted = case_when(
           P_value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
           P_value < 0.01 ~ sprintf("%.3f", P_value),
           TRUE ~ sprintf("%.2f", P_value)
         ),
         # Create a label that combines correlation and p-value
         label = paste0(Correlation_formatted, "\n(", P_value_formatted, ")"))

# Set the order based on clustering
melted_data$Species <- factor(melted_data$Species, levels = species_order)
melted_data$Food <- factor(melted_data$Food, levels = food_order)

# Create custom color palette
extended_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)

# Create the heatmap with values
ggplot(melted_data, aes(x = Food, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(extended_palette), 
                      limits = c(-0.15, 0.15),
                      oob = scales::squish) +
  # Add correlation and p-values as text
  geom_text(aes(label = label,
                color = abs(Correlation) > 0.1), # Make text more visible for higher correlations
            size = 2.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("black", "white"), guide = "none") +
  # Add asterisk for significant correlations
  geom_text(data = subset(melted_data, Significant), 
            aes(label = "*"), 
            color = "black", size = 5, vjust = -0.8, hjust = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),  # Italicize species names
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Spearman Correlations between Species and Food Groups",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and BMI\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Food Groups",
    y = "Species"
  )

library(gt)

# Create a wide format table of correlations
cor_table <- melted_data %>%
  dplyr::select(Species, Food, Correlation, P_value, Significant) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create a formatted value that shows correlation (p-value)
    formatted_value = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", Correlation),
      " (", 
      case_when(
        P_value < 0.001 ~ "p<0.001",
        TRUE ~ paste0("p=", sprintf("%.3f", P_value))
      ),
      ")"
    ),
    # Add asterisk for significant values
    formatted_value = if_else(Significant, paste0(formatted_value, "*"), formatted_value)
  ) %>%
  # Convert to wide format for the table
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = Species,
    names_from = Food,
    values_from = formatted_value
  )

# Create a nice looking table with gt
cor_table %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Correlation Coefficients and P-values",
    subtitle = "Spearman correlations between species and food groups (* indicates p < 0.05)"
  ) %>%
  fmt_markdown(columns = everything()) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(style = "italic"),
    locations = cells_body(columns = Species)
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.width = pct(100),
    table.border.top.style = "hidden",
    heading.border.bottom.style = "solid"
  )

# If you want to save both as separate files
ggsave("figure/correlation_heatmap_table.png", width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
cor_table %>% 
  gt() %>% 
  gtsave("correlation_table.html")

# Alternative: Save as Excel file
if (!require(writexl)) install.packages("writexl")
library(writexl)

# Convert back to long format for Excel export
excel_data <- melted_data %>%
  dplyr::select(Species, Food, Correlation, P_value, Significant) %>%
  arrange(Species, Food)

write_xlsx(excel_data, "figure/correlation_data.xlsx")
```



```{r}
# First calculate the overall clustering order from the full dataset
calculate_global_orders <- function(merged_data) {
  # Initialize matrices using full dataset
  species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
  food_cols <- colnames(food_data)[colnames(food_data) != "Participant_ID"]
  cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(food_cols))
  p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(food_cols))
  
  # Calculate overall correlations
  for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
    for(j in 1:length(food_cols)) {
      temp_data <- data.frame(
        species = rank(merged_data[[species_cols[i]]]),
        food = rank(merged_data[[food_cols[j]]]),
        gender = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
        age = rank(merged_data$FREG8_Age),
        bmi = rank(merged_data$BMI)
      )
      
      temp_data <- na.omit(temp_data)
      
      pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$food,
                             temp_data[,c("gender", "age", "bmi")])
      
      cor_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$estimate
      p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
    }
  }
  
  # Set row and column names
  rownames(cor_matrix) <- species_cols
  colnames(cor_matrix) <- food_cols
  
  # Calculate clustering order
  species_dist <- dist(cor_matrix)
  species_clust <- hclust(species_dist)
  species_order <- species_clust$labels[species_clust$order]
  
  food_dist <- dist(t(cor_matrix))
  food_clust <- hclust(food_dist)
  food_order <- food_clust$labels[food_clust$order]
  
  return(list(species_order = species_order, food_order = food_order))
}

# Get global orders
global_orders <- calculate_global_orders(merged_data)

# Modified function to create heatmap with fixed orders
create_ethnic_heatmap <- function(merged_data, ethnic_group, species_order, food_order) {
  # Filter data for specific ethnic group
  ethnic_data <- merged_data %>%
    filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic_group)
  
  # Print sample size for debugging
  cat("Sample size for ethnic group", ethnic_group, ":", nrow(ethnic_data), "\n")
  
  # Initialize matrices
  species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
  food_cols <- colnames(food_data)[colnames(food_data) != "Participant_ID"]
  cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(food_cols))
  p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(food_cols))
  
  # Calculate correlations
  for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
    for(j in 1:length(food_cols)) {
      tryCatch({
        temp_data <- data.frame(
          species = rank(ethnic_data[[species_cols[i]]]),
          food = rank(ethnic_data[[food_cols[j]]]),
          gender = as.numeric(ethnic_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
          age = rank(ethnic_data$FREG8_Age),
          bmi = rank(ethnic_data$BMI)
        )
        
        temp_data <- na.omit(temp_data)
        
        if(nrow(temp_data) > 10) {
          pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$food,
                                 temp_data[,c("gender", "age", "bmi")])
          
          cor_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$estimate
          p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
        }
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat("Error in correlation calculation for species", species_cols[i], 
            "and food", food_cols[j], ":", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
      })
    }
  }
  
  # Set row and column names
  rownames(cor_matrix) <- species_cols
  colnames(cor_matrix) <- food_cols
  rownames(p_matrix) <- species_cols
  colnames(p_matrix) <- food_cols
  
  # Convert to long format
  melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)
  colnames(melted_cor) <- c("Species", "Food", "Correlation")
  melted_cor$Significant <- melt(p_matrix)$value < 0.05
  
  # Use global orders for factors
  melted_cor$Species <- factor(melted_cor$Species, levels = species_order)
  melted_cor$Food <- factor(melted_cor$Food, levels = food_order)
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Food, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)),
                        limits = c(-0.2, 0.2),
                        oob = scales::squish,
                        na.value = "grey90") +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant & !is.na(Correlation), "*", "")),
              color = "black", size = 5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 8),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Ethnic Group:", ethnic_group),
      subtitle = paste0("n=", nrow(ethnic_data), "\nAdjusted for Gender, Age, and BMI\n* indicates p < 0.05"),
      x = "Food Groups",
      y = "Species"
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# Create heatmaps for each ethnic group using fixed orders
ethnic_groups <- c("C", "I", "M")
plots <- list()

for(group in ethnic_groups) {
  plots[[group]] <- create_ethnic_heatmap(merged_data, group, 
                                        global_orders$species_order, 
                                        global_orders$food_order)
}

# Combine plots using patchwork
library(patchwork)
combined_plot <- plots$C + plots$I + plots$M +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Stratified Correlation Heatmaps by Ethnic Group",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))
  )

# Save the combined plot
# ggsave("figure/figure4_stratified_correlation_heatmaps.png", 
#        combined_plot, 
#        width = 24, 
#        height = 10, 
#        dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
plots$C
plots$I
plots$M
# Save the combined plot
# ggsave("figure/figure4_stratified_correlation_heatmaps.png", 
#        combined_plot, 
#        width = 24, 
#        height = 10, 
#        dpi = 300)

# Save individual plots if needed
# for(group in ethnic_groups) {
#   ggsave(
#     paste0("figure/figure4_correlation_heatmap_", group, ".png"),
#     plots[[group]],
#     width = 12,
#     height = 10,
#     dpi = 300
#   )
# }
```

























```{r}
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(grid)
library(circlize)
library(RColorBrewer)
# Clip the correlation values to [-0.2, 0.2]
clipped_cor_matrix <- cor_matrix
clipped_cor_matrix[clipped_cor_matrix > 0.2] <- 0.2
clipped_cor_matrix[clipped_cor_matrix < -0.2] <- -0.2

#display.brewer.pal(11, "RdBu")  # This will show us the actual colors

rd_bu_colors <- rev(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))

# Modify color function to use full RdBu palette evenly over the actual data range
col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-0.2, 0, 0.2), rd_bu_colors[c(1, 6, 11)])

# Create the matrix of significance stars
star_matrix <- matrix("", nrow = nrow(cor_matrix), ncol = ncol(cor_matrix))
star_matrix[p_matrix < 0.05] <- "*"

# Create the heatmap using the clipped matrix
ht <- Heatmap(clipped_cor_matrix,
        name = "Correlation",
        col = col_fun,
        cluster_rows = TRUE,
        cluster_columns = TRUE,
        row_names_gp = gpar(fontface = "italic"),  # Italic species names
        column_names_rot = 45,  # Rotate column names
        cell_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
          if(star_matrix[i, j] == "*")
            grid.text("*", x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 14))
        },
        heatmap_legend_param = list(
          title = "Spearman\nCorrelation",
          at = seq(-0.2, 0.2, by = 0.1)
        ),
        column_title = "Food Groups",
        row_title = "Species",
        column_title_side = "bottom",
        show_row_dend = TRUE,  # Show row dendrogram
        show_column_dend = TRUE  # Show column dendrogram
)
ht
# Save the plot
png("figure/figure2_correlation_heatmap_with_dendrograms.png",
    width = 12, height = 10, units = "in", res = 300)
draw(ht, padding = unit(c(2, 2, 2, 2), "cm"))
dev.off()
```
